ZEAL/BZea DTA (days to anthesis) — MLM (Taxon + K) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel

The ZEAL reproduction of the TeoNAM DTA composite: JLM QTL, per-SNP vs RTIGER-ancestry MLM, and the ancestry↔︎flowering confound, on 1,403 B73×teosinte BC2S3 NILs

Author

Fausto Rodríguez-Zapata

Published

July 8, 2026

What this is. The ZEAL/BZea analog of teonam-qtl-recovery-dta-mlm-118k, built from real BZea data end-to-end: field DTA → SpATS spatial BLUEs → SNP50K teosinte-ancestry dosage → RTIGER ancestry mosaic → K → JLM + MLM. The population is 1,403 near-isogenic B73×teosinte BC2S3 lines from 5 teosinte taxa (Zx mexicana 573, Zv parviglumis 383, Zd diploperennis 254, Zl luxurians 118, Zh huehuetenangensis 61) — the analog of TeoNAM’s 5 TIL families. The DTA BLUE is the per-field SpATS mean across two fields (CLY23-D4, CLY25-B5; cross-year genetic corr r = 0.675).

ImportantGenotype at 0.4× skim: why ancestry, not per-SNP

SNP50K is 51,991 teosinte-vs-B73 sites pileup-genotyped at ~0.4× — hard genotypes are unusable (70% missing, ~0% hom-ALT), so ancestry is inferred from the per-sample allele counts. The authentic per-SNP dosage (Panel B) is therefore λ_GC-deflated (sparse mean-imputation); the RTIGER ancestry mosaic (Panel C) is the usable, block-smoothed genotype (teosinte presence 0.111, matching the established BzeaSeq ~0.101). This is the ZEAL-specific reason ancestry inference is mandatory — and the direct analog of the per-SNP-vs-block contrast in the TeoNAM notebook.

The central result mirrors TeoNAM: K = teosinte-ancestry relatedness is collinear with the flowering QTL, so the mixed model absorbs the very signal it protects — the flowering candidates (ZmCCT10, ZCN8) are suppressed under MLM. As in Chen 2019, the DTA QTL are therefore the province of JLM (nested-in-family stepwise), not the MLM Manhattan.

library(here)
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(fastman)
library(ggrepel)
library(ggtext)
library(scales)
library(cowplot)
source(here("scripts/teonam_notebook_plots.R")) # shared: plot_manhattan/lollipop, lambda_gc, LOD
mlm <- fread(here("data/zeal/dta_gwas_mlm_mosaic_donor_accession_snp50k.csv")) # Panel C
persnp <- fread(here("data/zeal/dta_gwas_mlm_persnp_donor_accession_snp50k.csv")) # Panel B
ols <- fread(here("data/zeal/dta_gwas_ols_donor_accession_snp50k.csv"))[is.finite(P) & P > 0]
overlap <- here("results/sim/zeal/dta_candidate_overlap.csv")
lam <- function(p) {
  p <- p[is.finite(p) & p > 0 & p <= 1]
  round(median(qchisq(p, 1, lower.tail = FALSE)) / qchisq(0.5, 1), 2)
}

Candidate genes: closest marker vs window-max marker

For each maize flowering candidate (B73 v5), the closest marker to the gene body vs the highest −log₁₀P marker within ±500 kb in the RTIGER-mosaic MLM (Taxon+K) scan (Panel C). The same v5 flowering candidates as TeoNAM (these are maize genes; teosinte introgression at them drives DTA in both populations).

sc <- copy(mlm)[is.finite(P) & P > 0][, logP := -log10(P)]
gg <- fread(here("data/teonam/dta_candidate_genes.tsv"))
gg[, chr := as.integer(chr)][, qtl := ifelse(qtl_chen2019 == "" | is.na(qtl_chen2019), paste0("DTA(chr", chr, ")"), qtl_chen2019)]
WIN <- 5e5
ctab <- rbindlist(lapply(seq_len(nrow(gg)), function(i) {
  ch <- gg$chr[i]
  s <- gg$start[i]
  e <- gg$end[i]
  m <- sc[CHR == ch]
  if (!nrow(m)) {
    return(NULL)
  }
  d <- pmax(0L, pmax(s - m$BP, m$BP - e))
  kc <- which.min(d)
  w <- m[BP >= s - WIN & BP <= e + WIN]
  kw <- if (nrow(w)) which.max(w$logP) else NA
  data.table(
    gene = gg$symbol[i], QTL = gg$qtl[i], chr = ch, `gene start (v5)` = s,
    `closest Δstart (bp)` = m$BP[kc] - s, `closest -log10P` = round(m$logP[kc], 2),
    `window-max -log10P` = if (nrow(w)) round(w$logP[kw], 2) else NA_real_
  )
}))
setorder(ctab, chr, `gene start (v5)`)
knitr::kable(ctab,
  format.args = list(big.mark = ","),
  caption = "DTA candidates (B73 v5): closest vs window-max (±500 kb) marker, RTIGER-mosaic MLM (Taxon+K) scan."
)
DTA candidates (B73 v5): closest vs window-max (±500 kb) marker, RTIGER-mosaic MLM (Taxon+K) scan.
gene QTL chr gene start (v5) closest Δstart (bp) closest -log10P window-max -log10P
zagl1 DTA(chr1) 1 283,849,414 12,915 0.65 0.76
zmads69 DTA(chr3) 3 161,172,047 29,298 0.30 0.57
zcn12 DTA3.2 3 202,794,887 3,509 1.45 1.63
tu1 DTA4.1 4 183,877,026 -2,231 1.16 1.16
zmm31 DTA5.1 5 7,374,462 -50,600 0.51 0.90
si1 DTA6.1 6 94,458,037 12,722 0.54 0.54
zag1 DTA6.2 6 142,692,209 65,043 0.47 0.66
dlf1 DTA7.2 7 184,528,042 -547,303 0.95 NA
zcn8 DTA(chr8) 8 126,678,681 -57,791 0.26 0.39
zmcct9 DTA(chr9) 9 120,960,326 7,897 0.25 0.31
zmcct10 DTA(chr10) 10 96,175,873 -2,308 1.64 3.28

OLS → MLM: the flowering confound and structure control

The fixed-effect OLS (Taxon + marker, no K) vs the MLM (Taxon + K) on the RTIGER mosaic, same fixed part. OLS is intrinsically inflated (ancestry-block LD, unfixable by any fixed factor); K deflates λ toward 1 and collapses the flowering peaks (ZmCCT10), the ancestry-QTL confound.

plot_manhattan(ols,
  sprintf("ZEAL/BZea DTA — OLS (Taxon + marker, no K), RTIGER mosaic (λ_GC = %.2f)", lam(ols$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, out_png = here("results/sim/zeal/dta_gwas_ols_manhattan_snp50k.png")
)

The OLS Manhattan (no K) is genome-wide inflated (λ_GC ≈ 2.4, ancestry-block LD) — the structure artifact the MLM K term removes below.

lam_tab <- data.table(
  model = c("OLS (Taxon + marker, no K)", "MLM (Taxon + K)"),
  markers = c(nrow(ols), nrow(mlm)),
  lambda_GC = c(lam(ols$P), lam(mlm$P)),
  max_logP = round(c(max(-log10(ols$P)), max(-log10(mlm$P))), 2)
)
knitr::kable(lam_tab, caption = "OLS vs MLM on the RTIGER mosaic (same Taxon fixed part): K deflates genomic inflation.")
OLS vs MLM on the RTIGER mosaic (same Taxon fixed part): K deflates genomic inflation.
model markers lambda_GC max_logP
OLS (Taxon + marker, no K) 48900 2.44 6.36
MLM (Taxon + K) 48900 1.19 9.61 
ov <- fread(overlap)
peak_at <- function(scan, ch, s) {
  w <- scan[CHR == ch & abs(BP - s) <= 5e5 & is.finite(P) & P > 0]
  if (!nrow(w)) NA_real_ else round(max(-log10(w$P)), 2)
}
cmp <- ov[, .(
  gene = symbol, chr,
  `OLS peak` = mapply(peak_at, list(ols), chr, start),
  `MLM peak` = mapply(peak_at, list(mlm), chr, start)
)]
cmp[, `drop (OLS−MLM)` := round(`OLS peak` - `MLM peak`, 2)]
setorder(cmp, -`OLS peak`)
knitr::kable(cmp, caption = "Local peak -log10P (±500 kb) per candidate: OLS vs MLM. K suppresses the ancestry-collinear flowering peaks.")
Local peak -log10P (±500 kb) per candidate: OLS vs MLM. K suppresses the ancestry-collinear flowering peaks.
gene chr OLS peak MLM peak drop (OLS−MLM)
dlf1 7 NA NA NA
zmcct10 10 4.95 3.28 1.67
si1 6 1.20 0.54 0.66
zmm31 5 1.07 0.90 0.17
tu1 4 1.06 1.16 -0.10
zcn8 8 0.82 0.39 0.43
zagl1 1 0.61 0.76 -0.15
zmads69 3 0.52 0.57 -0.05
zcn12 3 0.50 1.63 -1.13
zmcct9 9 0.38 0.31 0.07
zag1 6 0.13 0.66 -0.53

JLM QTL genomic distribution (Panel A)

DTA QTL from JLM (TASSEL StepwiseOLS, nested in taxon, enter/exit P=1e-4=LOD4). Model- independent — Chen’s primary DTA analysis and the correct home for these flowering QTL.

p_lolli <- plot_lollipop(
  here("results/sim/zeal/tassel/dta_jlm_native1.txt"), mlm,
  "ZEAL/BZea DTA — JLM QTL genomic distribution (nested in taxon)",
  overlap_csv = overlap, label_genes = c("zcn8", "zcn12", "zmcct9", "zmcct10", "dlf1", "tu1"),
  out_png = here("results/sim/zeal/dta_jlm_lollipop_snp50k.png")
)
p_lolli

MLM (Taxon + K), RTIGER ancestry mosaic (Panel C)

p_mlm <- plot_manhattan(mlm,
  sprintf("ZEAL/BZea DTA — MLM (Taxon + K), RTIGER ancestry mosaic (λ_GC = %.2f)", lam(mlm$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, out_png = here("results/sim/zeal/dta_gwas_mlm_mosaic_manhattan_snp50k.png")
)
p_mlm

Composite (A: JLM · B: per-SNP MLM · C: RTIGER-mosaic MLM)

Panel B (authentic per-SNP dosage) is λ_GC-deflated — the 70%-missing per-SNP panel at 0.4× is sparse — but catches sharp SNP hits (ZCN8) the mosaic block-terraces away. Panel C (the ancestry mosaic) is the usable, well-calibrated genotype. The B↔︎C contrast is the notebook’s per-SNP-scatter vs ancestry-block-smoothing, and the ZEAL demonstration that ancestry inference is mandatory at low coverage.

p_B <- plot_manhattan(persnp,
  sprintf("DTA — MLM (Taxon+K), authentic per-SNP dosage (λ_GC = %.2f)", lam(persnp$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap
)
p_C <- plot_manhattan(mlm,
  sprintf("DTA — MLM (Taxon+K), RTIGER ancestry mosaic (λ_GC = %.2f)", lam(mlm$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap
)
composite <- cowplot::plot_grid(p_lolli, p_B, p_C, ncol = 1, labels = c("A", "B", "C"), label_size = 20, align = "v", axis = "lr")
ggsave(here("results/sim/zeal/dta_composite_snp50k.png"), composite, width = 9, height = 13, dpi = 200, bg = "white")
composite

Multi-caller DTA scan (control · nNIL · RTIGER · LB-Impute · binhmm)

The ZEAL analog of the TeoNAM DTA five-caller composite: the same DTA MLM (Taxon+K) scan on each ancestry caller’s recovered genotypes, at the real ~0.4× depth (not a coverage sweep). The scientific content here is caller reliability at low coverage: the ancestry mosaic must be block/bin-aggregated — the two aggregated callers (RTIGER per-donor segments; binhmm 1 Mb bins) recover the correct teosinte fraction (~0.10, matching the established BzeaSeq), while per-SNP calling (control) and the fresh per-SNP HMM recalls (nNIL, LB-Impute) under-call and give λ-deflated scans.

callers <- c(control = "persnp", nNIL = "nnil", RTIGER = "mosaic", `LB-Impute` = "lbimpute", binhmm = "binhmm")
tb1 <- fread(here("data/teonam/dta_candidate_genes.tsv"))[symbol == "zmcct10"]
ctab2 <- rbindlist(lapply(names(callers), function(nm) {
  g <- callers[[nm]]
  sc <- fread(here(sprintf("data/zeal/dta_gwas_mlm_%s_donor_accession_snp50k.csv", g)))
  mf <- here(sprintf("data/zeal/zeal_%s_mosaic.rds", if (g == "mosaic") "rtiger" else g))
  pres <- if (file.exists(mf)) {
    st <- readRDS(mf)$state
    round(mean(st %in% c(1L, 2L), na.rm = TRUE), 3)
  } else {
    NA_real_
  }
  z <- sc[CHR == tb1$chr & abs(BP - tb1$start) <= 5e5 & is.finite(P) & P > 0]
  data.table(
    caller = nm, `teosinte presence` = pres, lambda_GC = lam(sc$P),
    `max -log10P` = round(max(-log10(sc[is.finite(P) & P > 0, P])), 2),
    `zmcct10 -log10P` = if (nrow(z)) round(max(-log10(z$P)), 2) else NA_real_
  )
}))
knitr::kable(ctab2, caption = "DTA MLM (Taxon+K) by ancestry caller at 0.4×. Aggregated callers (RTIGER, binhmm) recover teosinte presence ~0.10 (established); per-SNP (control) and per-SNP recalls (nNIL, LB-Impute) under-call and deflate λ_GC.")
DTA MLM (Taxon+K) by ancestry caller at 0.4×. Aggregated callers (RTIGER, binhmm) recover teosinte presence ~0.10 (established); per-SNP (control) and per-SNP recalls (nNIL, LB-Impute) under-call and deflate λ_GC.
caller teosinte presence lambda_GC max -log10P zmcct10 -log10P
control NA 0.42 37.53 1.37
nNIL 0.018 0.83 16.62 4.78
RTIGER 0.111 1.19 9.61 3.28
LB-Impute 0.035 0.73 19.89 1.38
binhmm 0.101 0.79 5.44 4.02 
panels <- lapply(names(callers), function(nm) {
  sc <- fread(here(sprintf("data/zeal/dta_gwas_mlm_%s_donor_accession_snp50k.csv", callers[[nm]])))
  plot_manhattan(sc, sprintf("DTA MLM (Taxon+K) — %s (λ_GC = %.2f)", nm, lam(sc$P)), overlap_csv = overlap)
})
cc <- cowplot::plot_grid(plotlist = panels, ncol = 1, labels = LETTERS[1:5], label_size = 18, align = "v", axis = "lr")
ggsave(here("results/sim/zeal/dta_caller_composite_snp50k.png"), cc, width = 9, height = 20, dpi = 200, bg = "white")
cc
